[CH12_CHAIR_TIMELINE_CONTRADICTION] In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:227, the passage yields only one coherent timeline: either the chair is explicitly post-fire in age, or the twenty-five-year continuity is attached to the place or habit instead of the chair. A reader should not be able to derive both claims from the same sentence.

findings.acceptance_test: After revising snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:231; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:233; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:235; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:237; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:239, a cold reader can tell Tommy is capable of violence and is moving toward a dangerous choice, but the passage does not explicitly state or paraphrase a murder method, holding Cliff underwater, or a fake-accident plan before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:245.
findings.problem: The chapter lands Tommy's danger once at lines 115-117, then keeps translating that turn into exact dock logistics and a near-explicit drowning scenario. That over-proves the beat: instead of a comic chapter carrying an edge the reader feels but may not yet name, the close makes the lethal intention easy to name and weakens the later tonal gear change.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the first violent register shift at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:115; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:116; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:117, but revise the closing planning passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:231; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:233; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:235; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:237; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:239 so Tommy's thought stays on Cliff as a concrete threat and on Tommy's bodily capacity, not on a fully articulated submersion or fake-accident scenario. Strategy options: cut the explicit 'kept him there' logic, replace the accident-engineering sentences with one or two pressure-bearing physical observations, or end on Tommy choosing proximity rather than method.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:227, the passage yields only one coherent timeline: either the chair is explicitly post-fire in age, or the twenty-five-year continuity is attached to the place or habit instead of the chair. A reader should not be able to derive both claims from the same sentence.
findings.problem: The chapter says Tommy is in the same chair he has used every September for twenty-five years, then in the same sentence says Martin bought the chairs the year after the 2002 fire. Those claims cannot both be true inside the established timeline: the specific Adirondacks are post-fire objects, not twenty-five-year constants.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:227 so the time claim matches the object's history. Either anchor the chair to the post-fire span, or separate the twenty-five-year continuity from the chair by making the repeated element the deck spot or ritual instead of this specific Adirondack.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:113; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:145; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:147; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:159; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:163; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:233; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:235, the prose still reads terse and forceful, but a line-by-line read no longer encounters repeated stand-alone fragment chains of the same form.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly falls into telegraphic fragment strings such as 'Craft beer. Leather loafers.' and 'The cabin. August 2002.' In this quantity they stop reading as selective pressure and start reading as a mechanical house tic. That weakens rhythm, clashes with the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration, and makes the chapter sound generated at the sentence level.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the fragment clusters at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:113; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:145; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:147; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:159; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:163; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:201; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:233; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:235 into grammatically complete sentences that keep Tommy's blunt, muscular cadence without relying on stacked noun fragments. Keep the concrete nouns and hard stops, but rebuild the emphasis through syntax and sequencing rather than dropped verbs or article-stripped beats.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3050; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3075 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.
findings.problem: Tommy's memory of the fire: bonfire outside, lighter fluid, fire jumped to porch, 'buddy's family place.' No document is referenced — this is recall, but it contradicts the document that supposedly records the same event.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3050; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3075. Establish one canonical version of the 2002 fire (date, accelerant, structure, victim location, document page count) and enforce it wherever the RT document is read on-page (chapters 8 and 16 must match exactly). Tommy's memory (chapter 12) and confession (chapter 17) may diverge from the document if the divergence is intentional and flagged as unreliable narration, but the two on-page readings of the same physical document must be identical.

findings.acceptance_test: The truck color is consistent, or the discrepancy is acknowledged as memory uncertainty.
findings.evidence: Ch8/2048 (Martin's document): 'His blue Ford pickup was still in the drive Friday night.' Ch12/2986 (Tommy's memory): 'Green Ford pickup. Dull paint. Mud on the wheel wells.'
findings.problem: Ray Toomey's truck is blue in Martin's written account (Ch8) and green in Tommy's memory (Ch12). The written account was composed days after the event and should be more reliable than a 22-year-old memory. If this is intentional unreliable memory, it is not signaled to the reader.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either match the truck color across both accounts, or have Tommy's narration acknowledge uncertainty about the detail ('green, maybe blue, the color was already fading in his memory').

findings.acceptance_test: Martin's described role in the fire aftermath is the same in both Ch8 and Ch16 versions of the document.
findings.evidence: Ch8/2058-2060 (Martin's document): 'Martin had driven back at three in the morning. Alone. Work gloves from the trunk. He repositioned the heater to make it look like it failed where it stood. He adjusted what he could of the spill pattern. The fire investigation ruled it accidental.' Ch16/4248 (Martin's document): 'The fire department ruled it electrical. Tommy told them what he told me. I didn't contradict him.'
findings.problem: In the Ch8 version of Martin's document, Martin returns alone to the scene at 3 AM and physically tampers with evidence (repositions the heater, adjusts the spill). In the Ch16 version, Martin merely stays silent ('I didn't contradict him'). This changes Martin's culpability from active evidence tampering to passive omission — a significant difference for the moral weight of the story.
findings.rewrite_direction: This is part of the same fire-document reconciliation as C01. Choose one version of Martin's role (active tampering or passive silence) and apply it consistently across Ch8 and Ch16.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
